programme.title: God's Country
programme.desc: A college professor gets drawn into an escalating battle of wills after she catches two hunters trespassing on her property in the remote mountains of the American West.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: Feral
programme.desc: A wild animal attacks six grad students on a weekend hike in the woods. One by one, they become infected with an unknown virus that turns them into bloodthirsty creatures.

programme.title: Stranded
programme.desc: Astronauts (Christian Slater, Brendan Fehr) come under attack by dangerous alien spores after a meteor collides with their moon base.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Strange events plague a troubled teen when she participates in a scientific experiment to stop her nightmares.

programme.title: Hellbender
programme.desc: A teen and her mother live simply in a home in the woods, spending their time making metal music. A chance encounter with a fellow teen causes her to uncover a connection between her family and witchcraft, which causes a rift with her mother.

programme.title: The Shed
programme.desc: A teen and his best friend endure nonstop torment from bullies at school, but that soon changes when one discovers a bloodthirsty creature that resides in a country shed.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A plane takes off during a snowstorm and soon reports both pilots are dead. As the passengers slowly die off, they wish they had never left the ground.

programme.title: Mary
programme.desc: From the writer of The Shallows. Evil lies just beneath the surface.

programme.title: Z
programme.desc: A boy's imaginary friend terrorizes his family.

programme.title: The Echo
programme.desc: Strange events plague an ex-convict (Jesse Bradford) when he moves into the sinister apartment of his late mother.

programme.title: Infected
programme.desc: Seven strangers try to escape from Los Angeles after a strange virus that mutates humans is released.
When Earth's air becomes unbreathable, a message from the future sends a man on a dangerous mission to an unknown world to save the human race.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: While making an instructional video, hunters become the prey after they trespass on government land.

programme.title: Brawl in Cell Block 99
programme.desc: A desperate former boxer takes work as a drug courier and winds up in a jail where his enemies force him to commit acts of violence.

programme.title: Mayhem
programme.desc: Fired from his law firm, a man and a former client must fight for their lives when a mysterious virus causes employees to become violent and aggressive. They join forces to reach the executives on the top floor and settle the score once and for all.

programme.title: Rogue Agent
programme.desc: Conman Robert Freegard poses as an undercover MI5 agent and kidnaps countless victims amidst a high-stakes manhunt, until the woman who fell for him brings him to justice.

programme.title: The Colony
programme.desc: Forced to live underground, survivors of an ice age embark on a mission to reach another outpost.

programme.title: ATM
programme.desc: A murderous assailant traps three people (Alice Eve, Josh Peck, Brian Geraghty) in a small kiosk containing an automatic teller machine.

programme.title: The Advent Calendar
programme.desc: An ex-dancer is given a mysterious advent calendar.

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: A same-sex couple moves to a small town with their 16-year-old daughter.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
programme.desc: In Samurai Town, a ruthless bank robber gets sprung from jail by a warlord whose adopted granddaughter has gone missing. Strapped into a leather suit that will self-destruct in five days, the bandit sets off on a journey to find the young woman.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Spare Parts
programme.desc: Weapons attached to one arm, four young women must fight like gladiators as a bloodthirsty crowd cheers them on.

programme.title: Black '47
programme.desc: In 1847 an Irish mercenary fighting for the British army abandons his post to seek revenge on those responsible for the death of his family.

programme.title: Fall Down Dead
programme.desc: A serial killer stalks seven strangers in an office building during a blackout.

programme.title: Day of the Mummy
programme.desc: Jack Wells arrives in Egypt to search for a legendary diamond, and his search leads him to the tomb of King Neferu. When Jack disturbs his centuries-old state, the mad pharaoh rises from the dead with a bloodlust that cannot be quenched.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the Cornish fishing village of Port Isaac. Behind the quaint facade lies something far more gritty - a place where people exploited a range of natural resources, on land and at sea, to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores.

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark visits the island of Inchmahome in Scotland, haven to Mary Queen of Scots, and ruminates on what it means to be Scottish.

programme.title: Crooks Anonymous
programme.desc: Dandy Forsdyke, a habitual criminal, is engaged to Babette, who wants him to go straight. Initiated into Crooks Anonymous, Forsdyke is guided to give up his criminal ways, but temptations abound.

programme.title: Being Stanley Baxter
programme.desc: TV and panto star Stanley Baxter had big success but an even bigger secret. With access to close family and friends, personal archive and recordings, we reveal his true story.

programme.title: The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn traces the story of the Romantic ballet and its greatest exponents. In 1832, the most famous ballerina in the world was Marie Taglioni. One of the first ballerinas to dance on the tips of her toes, she epitomised the Romantic age - ethereal and sylph-like. Her most famous role was La Sylphide. By mid-century, however, there were new styles, and it was left to the Russians to revive the Romantic age in 1909, when they brought Les Sylphides to Paris.

programme.title: Les Sylphides
programme.desc: A studio performance of the romantic ballet Les Sylphides, first broadcast on 3 April 1953 and starring Alicia Markova, John Field, Violetta Elvin and Svetlana Beriosova. Created by Michel Fokine and set to music by Frédéric Chopin, the ballet premiered in 1909 and was unusual at the time for being non-narrative, where grace and movement carry the piece rather than a storyline. The programme is introduced by renowned Russian prima ballerina Tamara Karsavina, who had danced in the first performance of the ballet in Paris on 2 June 1909. Les Sylphides is the first complete ballet in the BBC archive and was recorded by pointing a film camera at a television screen and filming the result (a method used for all recording until the arrival of video tape in the 1960s).

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Theatre and film director Lindsay Anderson in conversation with ballet superstar Rudolf Nureyev. First broadcast in 1974.

programme.title: The Classical Collection
programme.desc: The natural world has always been a powerful inspiration to composers. From vast forests and tiny fish to wild storms and epic seascapes, this programme takes us on an evocative journey through some of the best-loved musical responses to our living planet.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms looks at the handsome heroes and gorgeous heroines, pin-ups and bombshells who came to be known as cinema's greatest sex symbols. They were the actors who made audiences' hearts beat fastest - the men and women whom the camera just seemed to love that little bit more than most. The programme hears from the stars themselves talking about what their sex symbol status meant to them, and the good-looking line-up includes Gary Cooper, Jane Russell, Brigitte Bardot, Paul Newman, Robert Redford and Hollywood's ultimate sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe.

programme.title: Eve and Marilyn
programme.desc: 'She had a total understanding and control of the still camera... she was intelligent enough and sharp enough and beautiful enough to be able to manipulate almost any situation, so it became something which was totally her own...' Eve Arnold, one of the world's leading photo-journalists, remembers photographing Marilyn Monroe over the ten crucial years of Marilyn's stardom and decline. Going back to the original contact sheets and colour material, she tells of their friendship and what it was like as a woman to photograph Marilyn Monroe.

programme.title: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: A rare interview with former Hollywood sex symbol Jane Russell. She talks about her difficult childhood as the eldest and only girl of six siblings, her film career and working with Marilyn Monroe.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: It’s the penultimate match of the first round. Newnham College, Cambridge, face the Courtauld Institute of Art for a place in the second round. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Semi-final of the quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take players so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: The hidden wonders of India's spectacular natural world are revealed by wildlife expert Liz Bonnin, actress Freida Pinto and mountaineer Jon Gupta. Experience a village of birds, masks that come alive, the world's greatest mountain range and baby turtles erupting out of the sand. This is truly a land like no other.

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: In the final instalment of Andrew Graham-Dixon's windswept journey through the art of the Norselands, we arrive in Sweden - home of Ikea and a tradition of brilliant furniture design stretching back to the early years of the 20th century. Sweden has made its modern democratic mission one of comfort and civilised living for the masses - but has it got there?

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes charts the foundation of a national identity that sought architectural inspiration from Greece and Rome. He also looks at America's earliest major painters.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: An explosion of new voices from across the British Isles reinvents the arts, creating a richer, more diverse culture.

programme.title: King Otto and Football’s Greek Gods
programme.desc: In the summer of 2004, audiences looked on in disbelief as the Greek national football team, who had never previously won a single match or even scored a goal in a major tournament, took down the giants of world football to become the unlikeliest of European champions. The architect behind this unprecedented triumph was legendary German football coach ‘King’ Otto Rehhagel. After accomplishing every major success in Germany, he made the bold decision to leave all he knew behind and work in a foreign country with the underachieving Greek national team. This is the story of how these two contrasting cultures came together to speak the same language and write a new chapter of Greek mythology.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Semi-final of the quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take players so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: With Frank's wedding impending, Timothy sets his cap at bridesmaid Liz. Will our hero get his head out of the railings in time? Will he forget the ring? And if he gets married, will Mother come on the honeymoon?

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The French Revolution sent shockwaves through Britain. While some watched transfixed, others were horrified. Simon Schama explores why the British proved immune to the siren call of liberty, equality and fraternity.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Norma Jeane attends an audition at 20th Century Fox, where the head of casting suggests she change her name to Marilyn Monroe. After connecting with Joseph Schenck, Marilyn is able to get a job at Columbia Pictures, where she receives her first starring role. Upon renewal of her contract, the head of the studio makes an unwelcome advance which leads to Marilyn’s dismissal.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn’s rise is nearly ruined when news breaks that she posed nude for a calendar earlier in her career. She uses this to her advantage to showcase her body confidence. Marilyn demonstrates her unique talent for comedy in Gentlemen Prefer Blondes and reaches superstar status whilst using her position to push for pay parity.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn's forging a new identity for herself in NY, but when news breaks that she plans to create her own production company, Darryl Zanuck refuses to let her out of her contract.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn shines in her most celebrated comedy yet, Some Like It Hot. In the weeks before her death, Marilyn is on the cusp of a new and exciting future, making front pages with her skinny dip portraits and securing a triumphant new deal with 20th Century Fox.

programme.title: Being Stanley Baxter
programme.desc: TV and panto star Stanley Baxter had big success but an even bigger secret. With access to close family and friends, personal archive and recordings, we reveal his true story.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: For Talking Movies presenter Tom Brook, April 2026 marks a momentous milestone. It will be the 50th anniversary of Tom joining the BBC as a trainee news journalist. Over the decades, from London, Berlin and mostly New York, he has reported on culture and cinema in thousands of stories and programmes broadcast on the BBC. Tom has interviewed countless leading figures in cinema, from early pioneers like Bette Davis and Jimmy Cagney up to contemporary star names, including Timothee Chalamet, Meryl Streep and Lady Gaga. Tom has also met US presidents and UK prime ministers, and leaders in global culture from Bollywood to LA. In a special edition of Talking Movies, he looks back at his career and the actors and filmmakers he has encountered over 50 years. He reflects on how the film industry has changed over the last five decades and how, in very tumultuous times, he believes cinema is more relevant and useful to our lives than ever before.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, analyses whether there has been a significant shift in the relationship between the United States and China, examines the long-term political turmoil in the United Kingdom, and looks at the psychological impact of Artificial Intelligence.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI Decoded examines a dramatic week for Elon Musk that could reshape the future of artificial intelligence. A US court has rejected Musk's claims against OpenAI, bringing a long-running feud with co-founder Sam Altman into sharp focus and offering a rare glimpse inside one of the world's most influential AI companies. At the same time, SpaceX is preparing for what could be the largest stock market listing in history - a move that may value the company at up to $1.75 trillion and make Musk the world's first human trillionaire.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Rachel indulges her passion for the Swedish outdoors, inspired by both the woodland and the water. Whilst visiting the Swedish Archipelago, Rachel shows off her fishing skills and gets into the Scandinavian way of life cooking a perch-style wrap on an open fire at the waterside by a traditional Swedish cabin. Back home, Rachel recreates Swedish favourites with her very own Khoo twist. She makes a savoury Swedish celebration cake, known as smörgåstårta, a wild mushroom soup with dill fish balls and an edible forest floor.

programme.title: The President's Path
programme.desc: Caitríona Perry, Sumi Somaskanda and Bernd Debusmann look at why President Trump - just back from China - is suddenly turning his attention to Cuba. The Department of Justice has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro, the CIA director has flown to Havana for the first time in modern memory and secretary of state Marco Rubio is making the case for action 90 miles off the US coast. But with polls showing Trump's worst approval ratings of either term, gas prices biting swing voters and an expensive primary "revenge tour" targeting Republicans who crossed him, is the Cuba pivot the win the White House is hoping for - or another distraction from the issues that will decide the midterms?

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI Decoded examines a dramatic week for Elon Musk that could reshape the future of artificial intelligence. A US court has rejected Musk's claims against OpenAI, bringing a long-running feud with co-founder Sam Altman into sharp focus and offering a rare glimpse inside one of the world's most influential AI companies. At the same time, SpaceX is preparing for what could be the largest stock market listing in history - a move that may value the company at up to $1.75 trillion and make Musk the world's first human trillionaire.

programme.title: Eye Investigations
programme.desc: BBC Eye investigates the emergence of Russia’s largest nationalist group. These vigilantes are taking the law into their own hands – targeting migrants and those who go against ‘traditional values’.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: The Earth is warming due to climate change. But how hot could it really get? Author Kate Marvel discusses her life in climate modelling, and how climate science can't be separated from human emotions.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Lighthearted discussion of the day's news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: The guests on the show are chief secretary to the prime minister Darren Jones MP, former Labour cabinet minister Alan Milburn, and Reform UK treasury spokesperson Robert Jenrick MP. On the panel are children's commissioner Rachel de Souza, journalist Vicky Spratt, and James Reed, chairman and CEO of Reed Group.

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate.

programme.title: Pentecost
programme.desc: A celebration of Pentecost, exploring how people can respond to the call of the Holy Spirit in different ways to transform self and community. In a service from Hope Street Church in the city centre of Wrexham, north Wales, members of the congregation share inspirational stories of finding their faith alongside reflections of how this new and growing church is playing a part in local community regeneration. The service is led by Andy and Rachel Kitchen, with music from Hope Street’s worship band.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A young couple buy their first home together in Haydock and transform the three-bedroom semi-detached house into a stunning place for them to live. A developer in Blackpool restores a fire-damaged house back to a home for the rental market. And a family create a fantastic four-bedroom house from a small cottage in a picturesque Norfolk village.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: After 52 years and nearly 2,000 episodes, this is the last Football Focus. Alex Scott and guests preview the final day of the Premier League and look back fondly at the longest-running football magazine show in the world.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Bristol to explore the spiritual inspiration of John and Charles Wesley, and to introduce classic Wesley hymns from the oldest Methodist building in the world.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television shows. Email your thoughts to pov@bbc.co.uk

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provides support as well as trained assistance dogs to help those living with deafness or hearing loss in the UK.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Perth and Kinross, helping a young couple with a budget of £350,000 to find an ideal country home for them and their two young sons. Jules also meets a group of keen stand-up paddleboarders who do their bit to keep the waterways free of litter.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Wild West, where survival demands grit and adaptability. In snowbound Yellowstone, coyotes use skill and geothermal heat to survive. In Colorado, hundreds of rattlesnakes emerge after winter, while a pregnant female risks starvation to give birth. In California, acorn woodpeckers battle for the best food storage sites, using barns and trees to secure their winter supplies. In Arizona’s desert, honeypot ants survive extreme heat by storing nectar inside their own bodies deep underground. On the Great Plains, ageing bison face fierce rivals to win mates, while fires renew the grasslands. In Kansas, wild horses run through lightning-charged skies. On Nebraska’s Platte River, half a million sandhill cranes converge, their courtship dances creating a rare natural spectacle, now captured from above. The Wild West remains a place of extremes, where each creature must fight to endure its challenges.

programme.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
programme.desc: In 1976, 11-year-old Gru dreams of becoming a supervillain. Ecstatic when he receives a message from the supervillain team the Vicious 6, Gru finds himself the object of their wrath and needs all his wits and help from his minions to escape their wicked clutches.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, latest drama, analysis, reaction and interviews as the Premier League season concludes with plenty still to play for.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Adam Frost look back at the highlights of the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026. They showcase the must-have plants, the award-winning gardens, the latest design trends for your garden, and the famous faces that have visited the world’s greatest gardening event this year.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Firstsite contemporary art gallery in Colchester for its final visit, where finds include a creepy goblin figurine, a 17th-century amulet and porcelain plaque discovered buried in a box bought at auction. Clive Farahar finds a collection of signed Tolkien books dedicated to a man whose relatives were neighbours of the author, while Mark Hill is challenged to settle a family argument over the authenticity of a bronze figurine. The guitar responsible for the distinctive sound of British bands in the 1960s delights pop culture expert Claire Tole-Moir, and Ronnie Archer Morgan is captivated by a Congolese wooden shield that was previously used to pay off a debt. Siobhan Tyrrell explores a collection of items from the YMCA that reveal the bravery of female volunteers during the First World War while Will Farmer is thrilled to meet the owner of an exceptional Hans Coper vase, originally gifted by the celebrated ceramist himself. Alexandra Gill discovers a painting that may or may not be by the renowned equestrian painter Sir Alfred Munnings, while Fiona Bruce is joined by Lee Young to learn about state-of-the-art techniques in ceramic repairs.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Following the death of a chef in an arson attack on his restaurant, John and Janie come to a small fishing village to scrutinise the locals.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: With the England team at an all-time low after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland, Gareth Southgate becomes the new manager. Haunted by his own penalty miss with England, he inherits a broken team and a jaded fanbase. With psychologist Pippa Grange, he sets about changing the team’s mindset and tackling his and England’s biggest fear – penalties.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the final day of the Premier League season's ten matches, including Arsenal’s trip to Crystal Palace and Manchester City versus Aston Villa. Elsewhere, Spurs host Everton, and West Ham welcome Leeds to the London Stadium. Presented by Kelly Cates, with Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney.

programme.title: Football Eras: Andy Robertson meets Kenny Dalglish
programme.desc: Ahead of his final game for Liverpool, Andy Robertson catches up with Sir Kenny Dalglish and BBC Sport's Kelly Cates in a special interview. The left-back reflects on his career at Liverpool and previews Scotland's first World Cup in 28 years.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Jonathan Ross, Joanna Page, Harry Redknapp, Joe Swash, Susan Wokoma and Shazia Mirza.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: The Scarecrows' Wedding
programme.desc: An enchanting story about two devoted scarecrows, Betty O’Barley and Harry O’Hay, who are planning a wedding to remember. When Harry insists on leaving the farm to collect one last thing for their big day, a smooth-talking scarecrow named Reginald Rake swoops in with plans of his own. Chaos ensues, and soon, Betty is in danger and everything’s at risk. Can Harry get back in time and save the day? Full of heart, humour and adventure, this is the joyful tale about loyalty, love and learning that the most important thing isn’t a grand gesture – it’s simply being together. This half-hour animated special features an all-star voice cast with returning favourite Rob Brydon (Gavin and Stacey, The Trip) voicing Reginald Rake, Jessie Buckley (Wicked Little Letters, Wild Rose) as Betty O’Barley and Domhnall Gleeson (About Time, The Paper) as Harry O’Hay, all led by Sophie Okonedo (Slow Horses, Hotel Rwanda) as the narrator.

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Oscar-winning claymation comedy thriller from Nick Park, featuring the popular inventor and his dog. Wallace's whirlwind romance with the proprietor of the local wool shop puts his head in a spin, and Gromit is framed for sheep rustling in a fiendish criminal plot, meeting a sheep named Shaun along the way. Will Wallace snap out of his daze in time to rescue his canine companion?

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team investigate the fake bank accounts all registered in one Cheshire street and ask whether the bank responsible does enough to stop scammers opening accounts. Also on the programme, the woman who received a £600 high-end smartphone as a gift, only to discover it was counterfeit – and the online retail giant that wouldn’t refund her. And the quick-witted viewer who recorded a scam call and kept the criminals talking so he could expose their breathtaking tactics.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is charmed by a 19th-century cottage just outside Birmingham, and in particular the old-fashioned phone left behind. There is plenty of room for Tommy to park at a row of garages in London, and Jacqui is in Ipswich, where a tiny shower just off the kitchen can’t be passed off as being quirky!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha learns about England’s oldest biscuit, and the reds and blues shop for bargains in Nottinghamshire. Which team will have the recipe for success?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie goes back to the classroom, inspired by caring for her dad. Nerves hit on day one, but her first on-the-job work placement has a real impact.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson spends this weekend break in the company of sporting legend Steve Cram. The middle-distance runner and broadcaster joins Robson on a boat trip from Seahouses to the Farne Islands to watch seals and seabirds. The duo take to the beach to sample the practice of ‘walking meditation’ as they create labyrinths in the sand. The mini break ends in a session of green woodworking at a bush craft site near Northumberland National Park.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from urban Hertfordshire with a £550,000 budget have their sights set on moving to the Somerset countryside, where Jules Hudson has some splendid properties lined up to tempt them. However, Jules has a big surprise up his sleeve, as he questions not the type of new home they’re looking for but the location of it.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team take on two gardens. Charlie and Amelia are in Stockport to transform Christine and Mike’s unloved plot into a glamorous art deco, 1920’s-themed garden, dripping in Hollywood sophistication with a nod to California, its palm trees and exotic planting. Flo and Lee are in Bristol to convert Emi and Song’s awkwardly shaped wrap-around garden into a Japanese-British fusion garden. They would love the garden to draw on their combined Japanese and British heritage, so it’s a mix of planting with cherry trees, acers from Japan and English roses, with areas for their children to play, grow vegetables and socialise.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, friends Jamie, Liam, Jack and Dan – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money, step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns, but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Rose Matafeo. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie goes back to the classroom, inspired by caring for her dad. Nerves hit on day one, but her first on-the-job work placement has a real impact.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max confronts the Mitchells about his suspicions, Gina gives Eddie the cold shoulder, and Zack gets some unwelcome news.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B, determined to get a glowing review, whatever the cost. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their anniversary break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the couple make the most of their weekend away. Mary reveals she has never had a holiday, so Pat tells her about the joys and pitfalls of week on the Costa Del Sol. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison’s discarded portrait of him in the basement, are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on his every word. The Captain is outraged by this meaningless reverence, believing that only a handful of great war leaders deserve such unadulterated veneration. As he and Thomas clash over the Plaguers, he challenges them to endure a poetry recital in order to test their loyalty.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return in an episode packed with some of the best bits from series 19. A stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Among the guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are Lucy Beaumont, Bob Mortimer, Jamelia, John Kearns, Harriet Kemsley, Yinka Bokinni, Gyles Brandreth, Dianne Buswell, Mark Chapman, Montell Douglas, Tasha Ghouri, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Will Kirk, David Morrissey, Josh Pugh and Holly Willoughby.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: After a breakthrough World Cup reignites belief, the England team return to a tournament on home turf, amidst the Covid pandemic. As the pressure to win increases, Pippa Grange questions her role. Meanwhile, Gareth tries to unite a divided country ahead of Euro 2020, where he hopes the team finally bring it home at Wembley.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts, with guest panellists Phil Wang and Judi Love joining team captains Ian and Paul as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Arthur the King
programme.desc: Over the course of ten days and 435 miles, an unbreakable bond is forged between pro adventure racer Michael Light and a scrappy street dog companion dubbed Arthur. Desperate for one last chance to win, Michael convinces a sponsor to back him and a team of athletes for the Adventure Racing World Championship in the Dominican Republic. As the team is pushed to their outer limits of endurance in the race, Arthur redefines what victory, loyalty and friendship truly mean. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Morning Live is BBC One’s flagship daytime programme, bringing you the best advice from familiar faces across the BBC, original journalism and investigations, and a sparkle of Strictly magic

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team hear the incredible story of one Edinburgh woman left devastated when an online romance turned out to be a scam – with a sophisticated AI trick luring her into parting with thousands of pounds. We also go behind the scenes with the banking team charged with breaking the scammers’ spell in order to stop their unsuspecting customers from handing over their life savings. And Scam Interceptors’ Nick Stapleton reveals how so-called hustle kingdoms power a sinister global industry of romance scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two dog-loving developers tackle epic projects in Wales and Norfolk. Also, a seasoned developer is looking to move quickly and economically in Nottingham.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of carers compete to make the most profit, as twin sisters take on a mum-and-daughter duo at a bustling antiques fair in Newark.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie speaks to a friend about caring for a child with cancer before joining community nursing in action - and faces her own fear, needles!

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by his uncle Matheson as the pair head to the Scottish Borders for a weekend in the outdoors to relax and reset. On their way to their favourite fishing spot in Coldingham, they have a go at hedge laying, before Matheson relives his youth riding horses on the beach.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a young couple search for their first home together in the Oxfordshire countryside. They are hoping for space to accommodate a rabbit palace and an impressive collection of houseplants. Alistair also pays a visit to the University of Oxford Botanic Garden, where he discovers how nature is inspiring exciting new technological developments.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo and Joe are in Stockport to help Sasha and Rick transform their long, muddy, unloved garden into a wild English wonderland. With unsafe concrete steps making the patio hard to access, it’s no place for daughter Jocelyn or the new baby on the way, and hosting friends and family is a real challenge. The couple dream of a vibrant, wildlife-friendly space, bursting with colourful flowers and plants, where their children can grow up with adventure and magic all around. They want a practical design too, with a clear divide between patio and grass so that their rescue dog Nelly can roam without tracking mud indoors. Having spent the last 18 months renovating their home while raising Jocelyn and preparing for their second child, Sasha and Rick are ready for their garden to finally match their family’s energy and joy. Can the team deliver their dream sanctuary on a £4,000 budget?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four take on the Bridge of Lies. In this episode, it's family and friends Lynne, Derek, John and Kenny. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money, step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns, but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Rose Matafeo. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, and Eddie and Harry clash.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: It’s the quarter final, and Alan and the designers head to the UK’s 'town of books', Hay-on-Wye, in Wales, to transform two high-street shops. One is a specialist guitar shop and the other a gift shop featuring local arts and crafts. Working in pairs, they need to improve layout, footfall and visual merchandising before facing critiques from Michelle and retail expert Ross Bailey.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Zoe Ball follows in her ancestors’ footsteps in a journey that takes her from Scotland to Cornwall as she uncovers stories of resilience and the importance of family over place.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2010. Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved.

programme.title: The Photograph
programme.desc: Love stories set in the past and the present intertwine when the finding of a photograph leads an estranged daughter on a journey of discovery into her late mother’s early life.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet has become a PCSO and tries to gain Gaz's respect by making her first arrest, Louise is worried that Janet is a better mother than her, Donna and Gaz are both distressed when they receive their divorce papers, and Wesley tries to fit in with the gang by learning what he sees as the Runcornian way of life - eating pasties, shimmying like Tim and borrowing each other's clothes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna and Gaz have decided to get divorced and the decision takes them on a path down memory lane. They look back at their relationship in a series of fantasies and flashbacks, including the time they first met one another at the age of five, where we discover the reason for Gaz's fear of sheep. They also imagine how life would be without one another. Donna wonders what she and Louise will be like as old ladies and Gaz imagines how fat he may have been if they'd never met. This terrifies them and makes them wonder if they have actually made the right decision to divorce.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor turns bank robber when he is given a task he cannot refuse - steal from the most dangerous bank in the cosmos. With the help of a beautiful shape-shifter and a cyber-augmented gamer, the Doctor and Clara must fight their way past deadly security and come face to face with the fearsome Teller - a creature of terrifying power that can detect guilt.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: When terrifying events threaten Coal Hill School, the Doctor decides to go undercover. The Skovox Blitzer is ready to destroy all humanity - and worse, any second now, Danny Pink and the Doctor are going to meet.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lauren makes a risky decision, Jasmine, Oscar and Josh spend the day together, and Gina fears for her dad.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vicki and Grant try to stage an intervention, Mark grows desperate, and Josh makes Oscar jealous.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Walford gathers for the day of Nigel’s funeral, Grant worries about Mark, and the Mitchells head off on a mission.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant drowns his sorrows, Nigel leaves Julie a final gift, while Lauren puts her family first.

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. Now, for the first time, she opens up about the heartbreaking effects cyberbullies have had on her life. In this intimate, personal documentary, Jesy exclusively reveals the impact online trolls had on her mental health as she goes on a journey of rehabilitation. Allowing cameras a behind-the-scenes look at her personal and professional life, Jesy begins to understand the layers of protection she has built in order to cope with the bullies. To help her heal, she meets other victims of cyber abuse, sharing devastating stories of online trolling. Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy’s torment affected the dynamics within the band and how it impacted the group’s ability to perform. Jesy’s boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, offers a comforting support system to the pop star. He recounts falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced on social media. And members of Jesy’s family help her confront deep personal issues she's kept hidden for years.

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: Biopic of East End gangsters the Krays, following the lives of twin brothers Ronnie and Reggie, from childhood into adulthood, as they rose to prominence by terrorising the criminal underworld of 1960s London.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy Fury’s comeback begins in the shadow of his toughest year yet. With no ability to plan for the future and struggling with his identity, Tommy is at his lowest point personally and professionally, living alone in a house that was meant to be his and Molly-Mae’s forever home. For the first time, Tommy describes the day that Molly-Mae and Bambi left him and just how low he sank into the depths of alcohol. His role as a father to baby Bambi brings both joy and pressure as he tries to win Molly-Mae back and convince her he is a changed man. After finally getting the medical all-clear to fight again, Tommy and his trainer and father, John, prepare for his return to the ring against MMA fighter Darren Till. Team Fury travel down to London for the press conference, with John promising to be on his best behaviour - but when Till mentions Tommy’s brother Tyson, tensions erupt…

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Pride runs deep in the Fury family - and for Tommy, it’s both a legacy and a burden. As he takes his first tentative steps back into the spotlight, we learn how far his father, John, has gone in the name of protecting the family name - including time behind bars. That same fierce loyalty rears its head when Tommy prepares for his first gym appearance since announcing his comeback. But with fighter Darren Till throwing threats and chaos into the mix, John pulls Tommy out of the fight, unwilling to risk Tommy’s future career. It’s a crushing blow for Tommy, who’s desperate for momentum and purpose. Yet amid the pressure, there’s a flicker of hope - Tommy and Molly-Mae are spotted together on New Year’s Eve, with a kiss that hints at a brighter future.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy’s fight for redemption continues, but this time, it’s a battle for his own mind. Struggling to secure a new opponent, he knows a comeback fight is crucial for his mental health. Meanwhile, Molly-Mae’s new series trailer drops, pushing Tommy to face his own truths in the toughest interview of his life, where he opens up about his battle with alcohol addiction. Back in training, hope returns as a potential fight looms, leading Tommy and John to a career-defining meeting. For the first time in a long while, things seem to be looking up.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together, so things are finally looking up for him. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. Neither Tommy nor Roman share their father’s love of the outdoors but do their best to enjoy a night under canvas. Tommy is beginning to redecorate the house in the hope that Molly-Mae may move back in, and he does his first red carpet event since the break-up. At the event, he reveals that Molly has agreed to go on a trial family holiday with him to Dubai. Could they be returning reunited?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy and Jake are heading to Budapest to shoot a promo and hopefully finally close the deal for his next fight. After 18 months out of action, Tommy will return to the ring, and he can’t wait to get back to what he does best. Even better, brother Roman will be fighting on the undercard, so both the Fury brothers will be in the ring on the same night. Back in Cheshire, work on the house continues in preparation for Molly’s return. Meanwhile, Tommy has precious time with Bambi before he has to leave her to go into fight camp. On the day Tommy is supposed to be flying to Hungary to start training, the money still hasn’t reached his account, so John calls everything off. Four days later, the money has finally landed, and the boys are in Budapest, training hard in fight camp. On a rare day off, we discover the importance of faith for the Fury boys and how in particular it has helped Tommy through his tough times. On Easter weekend, Tommy is reunited with Bambi for the last time before he gets into the ring - will his hand survive the fight?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: It is Tommy’s birthday, but there is no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction, juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving he has still got what it takes in the ring. Fighting on the same bill as his older brother Roman, Tommy steps into the spotlight once again and puts himself to the test against a hard-hitting Bosnian heavyweight. In a high-stakes bout that could define his future, Tommy isn’t just fighting for his career - he is fighting for the life he is desperate to rebuild. Meanwhile, there is a more personal battle quietly playing out at home. With the house freshly prepared for Molly-Mae and Bambi’s return, Tommy hopes he has done enough to prove he has changed. But now that he may have won them back, can he keep them?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommy’s road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks’ total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldn’t train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity: a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi, and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to. In search of focus and inspiration, Tommy heads to Marbella to train with his childhood hero - legendary bodybuilder Dorian Yates. But in a world of yacht trips, sun-soaked distractions and luxury villas, can Tommy stay disciplined and keep his relationship and boxing career back on track? As doubts grow over his recovery, Tommy faces the toughest question of all - is this just a setback or the beginning of the end of his boxing career?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy’s recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommy’s next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mum out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair. It’s vital for Tommy’s mental health that he keeps physically active at all times, so he sets his sights on an ambitious new goal - a 100km triathlon. Meanwhile, John worries that the physical toll from this could jeopardise the chances of Tommy recovering and landing a big money fight. As Tommy throws himself into training for the triathlon, one question remains - can he avoid getting injured, or will this new challenge end his boxing career for good?

programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
programme.desc: In 1976, 11-year-old Gru dreams of becoming a supervillain. Ecstatic when he receives a message from the supervillain team the Vicious 6, Gru finds himself the object of their wrath and needs all his wits and help from his minions to escape their wicked clutches.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon and her team help Cherelle and Tony transform their chaotic two-bed home through a radical space-saving reorganisation.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex vanished on holiday with his mother and grandfather for six years. Now, he's retracing his steps across Europe to tell the full story for the first time.

programme.title: Blackmailed: The Sextortion Killers
programme.desc: Reporter Tir Dhondy investigates the rise of sextortion scams targeting teenage boys on social media, including the case of 16-year-old victim Evan Boettler. Her journey leads to Nigeria, where she confronts the scammers taking part in these crimes. With unprecedented access and interviews with industry insiders, Dhondy exposes a disturbing online underworld and its devastating consequences.

programme.title: Who Stole Tamara Ecclestone’s Diamonds?
programme.desc: Who is really behind Britain’s biggest ever burglary? The police, the victims and even the suspects talk for the first time about the £26m series of raids on celebrity homes.

programme.title: Hunting the Online Sex Predators
programme.desc: Are tech giants’ social media algorithms pushing harmful content while police are fighting a global cybersex crime wave?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy’s recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommy’s next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mum out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair. It’s vital for Tommy’s mental health that he keeps physically active at all times, so he sets his sights on an ambitious new goal - a 100km triathlon. Meanwhile, John worries that the physical toll from this could jeopardise the chances of Tommy recovering and landing a big money fight. As Tommy throws himself into training for the triathlon, one question remains - can he avoid getting injured, or will this new challenge end his boxing career for good?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since their separation last year, but John’s worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. He’s come a long way since he and Molly separated last year, and the truth hits home when he sees his face on a billboard - and shares the moment with Molly. The premiere brings together family and friends, all celebrating how far Tommy has come. But there’s no time to rest - he heads to the south of France for the triathlon, where Molly and Bambi surprise him at the finishing line. Even with the triathlon behind him, Tommy’s pursuit of physical challenges continues as he gears up for the Great North Run the following week. Riding a high, he is suddenly hit with a backlash on social media, leaving him to wonder what he can do to catch a break.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy’s childhood friend Chris asks him to do a book reading at the school where they once studied. Tommy faces his nerves and steps up, inspiring students to dream big and believe anything is possible. Tragedy then strikes with the passing of boxing legend Ricky Hatton - a devastating loss for his friends, family and Manchester. Tommy trained with Ricky for 18 months, and his friend and mentor’s passing reminds him how important it is to live life to the fullest. Tommy sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible: climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. Battling brutal conditions and exhaustion, he pushes through to finish with only minutes to spare, greeted at the summit by an emotional John. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed. He reflects on where he was last autumn and everything he’s achieved over the past year, and looks forward to a brighter future.

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: More and more luxury watches are being snatched off UK streets. In 2022, there was an estimated 60 per cent increase in watch thefts worldwide. It is now the crime of choice for many criminals, seen as easier and more lucrative than drug dealing. In this documentary, journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones’s £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Single, penniless poet Ruby has recently moved back home to live with her mum, Jen. When their strict Presbyterian neighbours descend on the home for her spoiled sister Eliza's engagement party, Ruby’s only job is to not shame the family...
